---
title: "1.2 Variables and Data Types"
subtitle: "Part 1: Variables and Type Choices"
format:
  clean-revealjs:
    slide-number: true
    css: unit1-shared.css
lang: zh-CN
---

## Course Directory

### Return to the course outline

[← Back to AP CSA / 返回课程目录](../../index.html)

## What Is a Variable?

### Memory location with a changing value

A <span class="term">variable</span> (变量) is a memory location in the computer that can store a value that can change or vary while the program is running.

For example, when you play a game, it will often have a score.

Scores often start at `0` and increase, so they can change. A score can be stored in a variable.

## Variable Example

### Pong score

::: {.image-fit}
![A pong game in Scratch with a score shown in the upper left](../../assets/1-using-objects-and-methods/1-2-variables-and-data-types/pongScore.png){fig-align="center" width="44%"}
:::

A score changes while the program is running, so it is a good example of data stored in a variable.

## Data Types

### Name plus type

Every variable has a name and a <span class="term">data type</span> (数据类型) that determines the kind of data it can hold.

There are two types of variables in Java:

::: {.tight-list}
- <span class="term">primitive variables</span> that hold values of primitive types like numbers
- <span class="term">reference variables</span> that hold a reference to a more complex object
:::

A reference is a way to find the object, like a UPS tracking number helps you find your package.

## Primitive Types

### AP CSA primitive types

The primitive types on the AP CSA exam are:

::: {.tight-list}
- `int`, which can represent integers: numbers with no fractional part such as `3`, `0`, `-76`, and `20393`
- `double`, which can represent non-integer numbers like `6.3`, `-0.9`, and `60293.93032`
- `boolean`, which can represent only two values: `true` and `false`
:::

## `double`

### Floating point numbers

Computer people call `double` values <span class="term">floating point</span> numbers because the decimal point floats relative to the magnitude of the number, similar to scientific notation.

The name `double` comes from the fact that `double` values are represented using <span class="mark">64 bits</span>, double the 32 bits used for the type `float`.

`float` is rarely used these days and is not part of the AP curriculum.

## `String`

### Reference type

`String` is one of the object types on the exam and is the name of a class in Java.

A `String` is written in a Java program as a sequence of characters enclosed in a pair of double quotes, like:

```java
"Hello"
```

You will learn more about `String` objects later.

## Data Type Domain

### Values and operations

A data type is a set of values, called a domain, and a set of operations on them.

For example, you can do addition operations with `int` values and `double` values.

You cannot do addition operations with `boolean` values and `String` values in the same numeric way.

## Quick Check

### `mchoice:: chooseType1`

What type should you use to represent the average grade for a course?

::: {.tight-list}
- A. `int`
- B. `double`
- C. `boolean`
- D. `String`
:::

Correct answer: <span class="mark">B. `double`</span>

An average is calculated by summing values and dividing by the number of values. To keep the most information, this should be done with decimal numbers.

## Quick Check

### `mchoice:: chooseType2`

What type should you use to represent the number of people in a household?

::: {.tight-list}
- A. `int`
- B. `double`
- C. `boolean`
- D. `String`
:::

Correct answer: <span class="mark">A. `int`</span>

The number of people is a whole number, so using an integer makes sense.

## Quick Check

### `mchoice:: chooseType3`

What type should you use to hold the first name of a person?

::: {.tight-list}
- A. `int`
- B. `double`
- C. `boolean`
- D. `String`
:::

Correct answer: <span class="mark">D. `String`</span>

Strings hold sequences of characters like you have in a person's name.

## Quick Check

### `mchoice:: chooseType4`

What type should you use to record if it is raining or not?

::: {.tight-list}
- A. `int`
- B. `double`
- C. `boolean`
- D. `String`
:::

Correct answer: <span class="mark">C. `boolean`</span>

Java uses `boolean` for values that are only `true` or `false`.

## Quick Check

### `mchoice:: chooseType5`

What type should you use to represent the amount of money you have?

::: {.tight-list}
- A. `int`
- B. `double`
- C. `boolean`
- D. `String`
:::

Correct answer: <span class="mark">B. `double`</span>

The `double` type can be used to represent an amount of money because cents may require decimal values.

## Classroom Check

### A complete answer should include

::: {.tight-list}
- define a <span class="term">variable</span> as a memory location whose value can change
- explain that every variable has a name and a data type
- distinguish primitive variables from reference variables
- identify `int`, `double`, and `boolean` as AP CSA primitive types
- identify `String` as an object/reference type
- choose a type based on the values and operations needed
:::

## End

### Continue to Part 2

[Next: Declaring, assigning, and naming variables →](1-2-part-2-declaring-assigning-and-naming-variables.html)
